insights.claim: The population-weighted U.S. average for loneliness is 34.41%.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted mean across ZCTAs with estimates.

insights.claim: The most-deprived ADI tenth averages 1.9 points higher than the least-deprived tenth.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted ADI national-rank decile means; ecological, not causal.

insights.claim: Across ZCTAs, loneliness is most associated with black population share (Spearman rho=0.445, n=23,801).
insights.method_note: Spearman rank correlation; place-level association only.
Maximum ZCTA value; ties broken by population.

insights.claim: About 48.3 million people live in ZCTAs at or above the high-burden threshold (37.6%).
insights.method_note: Population in ZCTAs at or above the 90th percentile (37.6%).
